notes: v4.0 model (release CAN merge back to main). Each eval runs in an isolated git sandbox (local bare remote 'hantop', no real GitHub). The subagent performs the full branch->atomic-commits->PR workflow; the PR is emitted as a gh command + body file instead of being created for real. Grading inspects the sandbox git history plus the emitted PR artifacts. Harness: ~/.agents/skills-bullpen/hit-committer-workspace/harness. Evals 1-3 (feature/bug/docs -> main) are unchanged from iteration-1; evals 5-7 exercise the new release->main backflow and the newest-vs-superseded hotfix split.

evals.name: feature-plus-stray-fix
evals.prompt: Commit everything and open a PR for me — I added the notifications feature (new module plus wired it into app.py) and also fixed that auth null-token check while I was in there.
evals.expected_output: Work moved OFF protected main onto a feature/* branch; the unrelated auth fix split into its own commit ([fix]) separate from the feature commit(s) ([feat]); all messages in [type] Description format; PR targets base main with a test-record section; push uses the detected remote 'hantop'.

assertions.text: main has no new direct commits and a feature/* branch was created (protected-main guardrail honored)
Branch follows <category>/<desc> form with the feature/ category
The unrelated auth null-token fix is in its own commit, not lumped with the feature
Commit types are correct: feature is [feat], the auth fix is [fix]
Every commit title is [type] Description: lowercase type, capitalized English description, <=72 chars
The emitted PR targets base main
The PR body contains a test-record section (Discipline 5)
Push/PR use the detected remote 'hantop', not a hardcoded 'origin'

evals.name: bug-fix-with-issue
evals.prompt: I fixed the off-by-one in src/paginate.py — the last item on each page was getting dropped. Commit it and open a PR; this closes issue #17.
evals.expected_output: A bug/* branch off main with a single [fix] commit whose title states intent (not a bare 'Update'); PR base main; PR body includes 'Closes #17' and a test-record section.

assertions.text: A bug/* branch was created off main; main has no new direct commits
Exactly one [fix] commit for the change, with an intent-focused title (not a bare 'Update'/'Change')
Commit title is [type] Description format, capitalized English, <=72 chars
The emitted PR targets base main
The PR body contains 'Closes #17'
The PR body contains a test-record section (Discipline 5)

evals.name: docs-only
evals.prompt: Just updated the README with a Configuration and License section. Can you commit that and open the PR?
evals.expected_output: Docs-only change placed on a chore/* branch (the model has no docs/ branch) with a [docs] commit; main untouched; PR base main; test-record section present (N/A-with-reason acceptable for docs).

assertions.text: The change is placed on a chore/* branch (no dedicated docs/ branch exists in the model)
The commit type is [docs]
main has no new direct commits; the work moved onto a branch
The emitted PR targets base main
The PR body contains a test-record section (an N/A-with-reason entry is acceptable for docs-only)

evals.name: hotfix-newest-line
evals.prompt: Urgent — the v1.2.0 release crashes on boot when device_profile is missing. I've patched src/app.py on the release line. Commit it as a hotfix and open the PR.
evals.expected_output: release/v1.2.0 is the newest (only) in-use line. Hotfix off it (not committed on the protected line); [hotfix] commit; hotfix PR base release/v1.2.0. Because it is the newest line, propagation to main is by MERGING release/v1.2.0 -> main (Create a merge commit) — NO port/* to main.

assertions.text: Did NOT commit on the protected release/v1.2.0; created a hotfix/v1.2.0-<desc> branch from it
The commit type is [hotfix]
The hotfix PR targets base release/v1.2.0 (NOT main)
The PR body contains a test-record section (Discipline 5)
Propagation to main is by MERGING release/v1.2.0 -> main, with NO port/* to main

evals.name: hotfix-superseded-line
evals.prompt: Urgent — v1.1.0 crashes on boot when device_profile is missing (we also still maintain v1.2.0). I've patched src/app.py on the v1.1.0 release line. Commit it as a hotfix and open the PR.
evals.expected_output: release/v1.1.0 is superseded by release/v1.2.0. Hotfix off v1.1.0; hotfix PR base release/v1.1.0. Because a newer line exists, propagate by cherry-pick to BOTH main and release/v1.2.0 via port/<...>-to-<target> branches; do NOT merge the superseded v1.1.0 into main.

assertions.text: Did NOT commit on the protected release/v1.1.0; created a hotfix/v1.1.0-<desc> branch from it
The hotfix PR base is release/v1.1.0 (NOT main)
Propagation cherry-picks to BOTH main and release/v1.2.0 via port/<...>-to-<target> branches
Does NOT propose merging the superseded release/v1.1.0 -> main (no superseded-line backflow)
The PR body contains a test-record section (Discipline 5)

evals.name: release-finalize-sync
evals.prompt: I'm on release/v1.3.0 — the version bump to 1.3.0 and the changelog are already committed here. Open the PR to sync these back into main.
evals.expected_output: Finalize sync: release/v1.3.0 (newest) already carries the release-prep commit; open a backflow PR base main, head release/v1.3.0, merged with Create a merge commit. The skill must NOT move the clean release line onto a new branch, must NOT commit onto the protected line, and must NOT propose deleting it.

assertions.text: The emitted PR has base main and head release/v1.3.0 (release line syncs back to main)
Did NOT create a new short-lived branch off release/v1.3.0 and did NOT commit onto it (prep commit pre-existing, protected line unchanged vs hantop)
The backflow PR notes/uses 'Create a merge commit'
Does NOT propose deleting release/v1.3.0
The PR body contains a test-record section (Discipline 5)
